message:
feat(core): implement BudgetEnforcer for latency stage tracking (ft-2p9cb.1.1.2)

AARSP A1 Impl: runtime budget enforcement for the latency pipeline.

BudgetEnforcer:
- Sliding-window percentile estimation (configurable window size)
- Per-stage overflow detection against p50/p95/p99/p999 budgets
- Mitigation recommendations (Skip/Degrade/Shed/Defer) per stage/percentile
- Structured log emission (overflow-only or all observations)
- Diagnostic snapshots with per-stage stats and aggregate slack
- Pipeline run builder from accumulated observations

Supporting types:
- BudgetEnforcerConfig with default policies
- StageMitigationPolicy per stage
- ObservationResult with overflow + mitigation info
- PercentileSnapshot and EnforcerSnapshot for diagnostics
- LatencyWindow ring buffer for bounded memory percentiles

19 new BudgetEnforcer unit tests (69 total in module, all pass)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1","title":"[AARSP][Track A] Latency Physics Contract and Budget Enforcement","description":"## Track Goal
Establish a formal latency physics contract so responsiveness targets are explicit, measurable, and enforceable.

## Why This Matters
Without stage-level budget algebra, teams optimize locally and regress globally.

## Deliverables
- Latency budget decomposition model.
- Runtime budget enforcement and adaptive allocation.
- Operator-facing knobs and policy-safe defaults.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Every critical pipeline stage has explicit latency budgets and instrumentation.
2. Budget violations trigger deterministic mitigation paths with clear reason codes.
3. Contract is covered by unit and e2e tests with artifact-backed evidence.

## Test and Observability Plan
Unit tests for budget math and edge handling; e2e scripts for overload and recovery; structured latency traces and decision logs.

## User Impact
Operators get predictable response times and clear explanations when the system degrades gracefully.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.642999Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.732451Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","slo","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":446,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Future-self: if this track is weak, all downstream optimization tracks drift into heuristic tuning with unclear outcomes.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","title":"[AARSP][A1] Latency stage decomposition and budget algebra","description":"## Background
Reactive systems fail when latency budgeting is implicit.

## Objective
Define formal stage decomposition and budget algebra for input-to-visible-response path.

## Scope
- Enumerate stages and interfaces.
- Define p50/p95/p99/p999 targets per stage.
- Define composition rules and slack policies.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Budget model covers all critical stages and edge paths.
2. Composition math is validated by unit tests and adversarial cases.
3. E2E scenarios verify aggregate and per-stage budget accounting.
4. Structured logs include per-stage budget deltas and overflow reasons.

## User Impact
Predictable response behavior and faster diagnosis of latency hotspots.","notes":"BronzeGrove progress 2026-02-23: implemented deterministic latency contract + budget algebra in crates/frankenterm-core/src/latency_model.rs (QuantileBudgetMs, StageSlackPolicy, LatencyPathContract, evaluate_latency_budget, canonical input_to_visible_response_contract_v1) with adversarial unit coverage. Validation: rch-executed cargo fmt --check PASS; cargo check --workspace --all-targets PASS; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings FAIL due existing repo-wide clippy debt outside this bead. latency_model filtered tests: 78 passed.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.787922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:34:07.472726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency-math","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1214,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.1.1.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.1.1.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.1.1.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.1.1.2 -> ft-2p9cb.1.1.1 and ft-2p9cb.1.1.3 -> ft-2p9cb.1.1.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.1","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Spec] ft-2p9cb.1.1 :: Contracts, invariants, benchmark and test matrix","description":"## Objective\nTurn parent bead into a deterministic execution contract with measurable outcomes.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Boundary map for code paths, interfaces, and failure domains touched by the parent task.\n2. Explicit invariants and impossible-state definitions with reason-code taxonomy.\n3. Benchmark contract: workload classes, p50/p95/p99 targets, pass/fail criteria, and overhead budgets.\n4. Verification matrix: unit, property, integration, e2e, chaos, and soak scenarios including nominal/degraded/recovery cases.\n5. Structured logging contract (required fields): timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Spec is precise enough that two engineers implement the same behavior.\n2. Every success/failure mode has explicit test ownership.\n3. Benchmark and test matrix include edge-case and adversarial conditions.\n4. User-facing impact is mapped to measurable metrics and guardrails.\n\n## User Impact\nReduces ambiguity, prevents regressions, and accelerates reliable delivery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.643839Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:06:37.546843Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T20:06:37.546830Z","close_reason":"Spec complete: latency_stages.rs with 50 unit tests + proptest_latency_stages.rs with 25 property tests. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","planning","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.643839Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Impl] ft-2p9cb.1.1 :: Deterministic implementation and guardrails","description":"## Objective\nImplement the parent capability with deterministic semantics, bounded resource behavior, and explicit fallback policy.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Core implementation for planned fast/slow paths with explicit guardrails.\n2. Failure-handling and degradation ladder with stable reason codes.\n3. Performance-sensitive path optimizations validated against the benchmark contract.\n4. Operator-visible controls and diagnostics required by parent scope.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. Behavior matches the subtask spec contract and parent acceptance criteria.\n2. No feature loss and no silent behavior drift.\n3. Deterministic behavior under fixed input/seed/config for replay scenarios.\n4. Structured logs are emitted at all critical decisions and failure transitions.\n\n## User Impact\nDelivers real responsiveness/reliability gains without sacrificing predictability.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.719434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:19:45.182926Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","implementation","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.719434Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.876599Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.3","title":"[AARSP][Subtask][Verify] ft-2p9cb.1.1 :: Unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence gates","description":"## Objective\nProve correctness, performance, and resilience for the parent task with reproducible evidence.\n\n## Deliverables\n1. Comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, and failure conditions.\n2. Property/concurrency tests where applicable for invariants and race-sensitive behavior.\n3. Integration + e2e scripts with detailed scenario logging (nominal, stress, failure, recovery, soak).\n4. Artifact bundle: benchmark outputs, golden traces/fixtures, log manifests, and mismatch diagnostics.\n5. CI/promotion gate updates that fail on missing artifacts, budget regressions, or proof violations.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n1. All required test layers exist and pass reproducibly.\n2. Logs provide high-fidelity triage context and decision lineage.\n3. Evidence artifacts are machine-verifiable and retained for review.\n4. Parent task can be closed only after evidence gate criteria are met.\n\n## User Impact\nEnsures optimizations are safe, durable, and trust-preserving in production.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.797837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:19:45.443403Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","quality-gate","subtask","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.797837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:17:46.949129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","title":"[AARSP][A2] Budget instrumentation and correlation propagation","description":"## Objective
Implement nanosecond-grade instrumentation points and correlation propagation for budget accounting.

## Scope
- Timer probes at all stage boundaries.
- Correlation id propagation across async boundaries.
- Minimal overhead logging strategy.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Instrumentation adds bounded overhead with benchmark evidence.
2. Correlation chain is complete for end-to-end traces.
3. Unit + e2e tests validate correctness and missing-probe failure detection.
4. Logs/artifacts are CI-exported for every performance test run.

## User Impact
Operators can pinpoint why responsiveness changed instead of guessing.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:13:04.860Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T00:19:45.458987Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact","determinism","e2e-tests","extreme-optimization","latency","observability","performance","quality-gate","reactivity","reliability","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1191,"issue_id":"ft-2p9cb.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Execution split added for implementation rigor and testability:\n1. ft-2p9cb.1.2.1 (spec/contracts/benchmark/test matrix)\n2. ft-2p9cb.1.2.2 (deterministic implementation + guardrails)\n3. ft-2p9cb.1.2.3 (unit/property/integration/e2e + evidence/CI gates)\n\nDependency chain: ft-2p9cb.1.2.2 -> ft-2p9cb.1.2.1 and ft-2p9cb.1.2.3 -> ft-2p9cb.1.2.2.\nParent-child linkage keeps the subtask relationship explicit while preserving DAG integrity.","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:18:24Z"}]}
